description:
A native plugin declares wants_context in its DescribeResponse. When set, bl serializes the EventCtx — prior task projection (the diff), actor identity, per-invocation overrides used (e.g. --no-sync), event name, commit sha, repo identity — to a dedicated FD (follow the BALLS_DIAG_FD pattern in plugin/diag.rs) or a --ctx-file path argument. The legacy stdin shape (bare Task JSON) is untouched. A plugin that does not declare wants_context receives byte-identical input to today.

EventCtx already exists as an internal type on the Participant trait (S5); this is purely its wire serialization plus a versioned, additive schema — new keys ignored by older context-aware plugins, per the prereq's rule.

WHY: the post-image alone cannot answer "who changed what" or "what was it before". This is the single highest-leverage gap — it converts every observer plugin from "diff against my own shadow copy" to "react to the actual transition". Gating on describe + a dedicated FD is exactly what keeps it forward-compatible in BOTH directions: an old bl never sends it, an old plugin never asks for it.
title: Deliver EventCtx to native plugins via a describe-gated side channel
